results.note: One command-auth suite required the excluded external @openclaw/fs-safe package.
Four failures use POSIX executable paths on native Windows; the remaining policy/sandbox suites require @openclaw/fs-safe.

findings.title: OpenClaw is not safe-by-default as a SEED execution boundary
findings.detail: Static source inspection shows sandbox mode defaults to off when absent, while tools.exec security is documented as full on the normal host and deny only on the sandbox host.

findings.title: Full Windows runtime could not be evaluated
findings.detail: The installed Node version is below OpenClaw's required minimum and the benchmark intentionally avoided installing the full monorepo or starting its gateway.

findings.title: Heartbeat and session patterns are strong
findings.detail: Lifecycle cancellation, bounded session storage, parent ownership, channel allowlists, heartbeat cooldown, active hours, visibility, wake scheduling and message access passed the selected source-only suites.

verdict: Use OpenClaw only as the primary design reference for heartbeat and session lifecycle. Do not embed it as SEED runtime or trust it as a safety boundary with default configuration.
